title: Cross-language SDK signature inventory (canonical shape)
description: JSON Schema for the canonical signature inventory each port emits. The Python reference (signalwire-python) is the oracle; every other port's adapter normalizes its native API surface into this exact shape so that diff_port_signatures.py can compare against python_signatures.json. See ADAPTER_CONTRACT.md for the per-adapter contract that produces a document conforming to this schema.

version.description: Schema version. Bumped when the canonical shape itself changes (not when types or aliases change).

generated_from.description: Free-form string identifying the source — e.g. 'griffe 2.0.2' or 'rustdoc-types 0.x via cargo-public-api'. Optional; helps debugging adapter regressions.

modules.description: Map from canonical Python-style dotted module name (e.g. 'signalwire.core.agent_base') to its inventory. Ports MUST translate native module / namespace names to the Python reference's dotted form at adapter time so diffs line up.

classes.description: Map from canonical class name (PascalCase, matches Python reference) to its method inventory.

functions.description: Module-level (free) functions. Map from canonical function name (snake_case) to its signature.

methods.description: Map from canonical method name (snake_case) to its signature.

returns.description: Return type as a canonical type string (see type_vocabulary.yaml). Use 'void' for procedures with no meaningful return value.

name.description: Canonical parameter name (snake_case in Python reference). Adapters MUST translate native names (e.g. JavaScript camelCase) to the Python reference's snake_case at adapter time.

type.description: Canonical type string from type_vocabulary.yaml. Required for every parameter except the receiver itself.

kind.description: How the parameter is passed. 'self' for the implicit receiver in instance methods (Python self, Ruby self, .NET this). 'cls' for class-method receivers (Python @classmethod). 'positional' (default) for ordinary positional or positional-or-keyword params. 'keyword' for keyword-only params (Python after a bare *). 'var_positional' for *args. 'var_keyword' for **kwargs.

required.description: True if the parameter has no default and the caller MUST supply a value. Defaults to true if omitted.

default.description: Default value as a JSON-serializable representation, or null if the default is null/None. Omit entirely if the parameter is required (has no default).

allOf.comment: If kind == self or cls, type and default are not meaningful; required is implicitly true.
Every non-receiver param MUST have a type.

canonicalType.description: A canonical type string. Form: a primitive ('string', 'int', 'float', 'bool', 'bytes', 'datetime', 'any', 'void'), a parameterized type with angle brackets ('list<string>', 'dict<string,any>', 'optional<int>', 'union<string,int>', 'tuple<int,int>', 'callable<list<any>,void>'), or a class reference ('class:signalwire.core.agent_base.AgentBase'). The full enumeration of legal forms is in type_vocabulary.yaml. Adapters that emit a value not matching this grammar MUST fail loudly at adapter time, not silently emit 'any'.
